questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: 'By the time the rescue team arrived, the trapped miners ____ for nearly ten hours.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An action continuing up to a point in the past uses the past perfect continuous tense.

**Working.** The reference point ('the team arrived') is in the past, and the waiting was ongoing before it. This requires 'had been + verb-ing'.

**Answer.** had been waiting.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the correct article: 'The supervisor is ____ honest engineer who never compromises on safety.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The choice between 'a' and 'an' depends on the sound, not the spelling, of the following word.

**Working.** 'Honest' begins with a silent 'h', so it starts with a vowel sound, requiring 'an'.

**Answer.** an.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct preposition: 'The ore sample was analysed ____ traces of gold.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Analyse for' is the idiomatic collocation when testing for the presence of something.

**Working.** We test a sample 'for' a substance; the other prepositions do not fit this sense.

**Answer.** for.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct verb form: 'Neither the manager nor the workers ____ satisfied with the new shift schedule.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In 'neither ... nor ...' constructions, the verb agrees with the subject nearer to it.

**Working.** The nearer subject is 'workers' (plural), so the verb must be 'are'.

**Answer.** are.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Identify the grammatically correct sentence (free of a dangling modifier).
options: Walking through the tunnel, the engineer was protected by the helmet from falling debris.
Walking through the tunnel, the helmet protected the engineer from falling debris.
The helmet, walking through the tunnel, protected the engineer.
Walking through the tunnel, falling debris was stopped by the helmet.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The introductory '-ing' phrase must modify the subject that immediately follows it.

**Working.** Only the engineer can 'walk through the tunnel'; the helmet and the debris cannot. Hence the subject after the comma must be 'the engineer'.

**Answer.** 'Walking through the tunnel, the engineer was protected by the helmet ...'.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert to the passive voice: 'The committee approved the safety proposal.'
options: The safety proposal was approved by the committee.
The safety proposal is approved by the committee.
The safety proposal has approved the committee.
The committee was approved by the safety proposal.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Passive voice: object becomes subject, verb becomes 'be + past participle', and the tense is preserved.

**Working.** Simple past 'approved' becomes 'was approved'; the object 'proposal' moves to the front.

**Answer.** 'The safety proposal was approved by the committee.'

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the best connective: '____ the equipment was old, it functioned reliably.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A contrast between two clauses is introduced by a concessive conjunction.

**Working.** Old equipment working reliably is unexpected, so a contrast word ('Although') is needed; 'Because' would imply causation.

**Answer.** Although.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct pronoun: 'It was ____ who first reported the gas leak.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** After the linking verb 'was', a subject complement takes the nominative (subject) case.

**Working.** The pronoun renames the subject 'It', so the nominative 'he' is correct (not the objective 'him').

**Answer.** he.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Identify the part that contains an error: 'One of the pumps (A) / are not working (B) / properly in (C) / the lower level. (D)'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The phrase 'one of the + plural noun' takes a singular verb because the true subject is 'one'.

**Working.** The subject is 'one', so the verb should be 'is not working', not 'are not working'. The error lies in part B.

**Answer.** B.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the word most similar in meaning to 'ABATE'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Abate' means to become less intense or to reduce.

**Working.** Among the options, 'diminish' is the closest synonym; 'increase' is its antonym.

**Answer.** diminish.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the synonym of 'METICULOUS'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Meticulous' describes someone who shows great attention to detail.

**Working.** 'Careful' captures this meaning; the remaining options describe the opposite trait.

**Answer.** careful.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'AUGMENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Augment' means to make larger or increase.

**Working.** Its antonym is 'diminish'; the other three are synonyms of augment.

**Answer.** diminish.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The idiom in 'The new exploration plan was a shot in the dark' means:
options: a wild guess with little chance of success
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Idioms carry a figurative meaning distinct from their literal words.

**Working.** 'A shot in the dark' is an attempt made without enough information — essentially a wild guess.

**Answer.** a wild guess with little chance of success.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: 'After the long shift, the foreman decided to call it a day.' This means he decided to:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Call it a day' is an idiom meaning to stop an activity for the time being.

**Working.** In context, the foreman chose to end work for the day.

**Answer.** stop work for the day.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt and appropriate word: 'The new regulation will ____ the older guidelines.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Supersede' (to replace and render obsolete) is the correct word and spelling.

**Working.** 'Supercede' is a misspelling; 'surpass' means to exceed, and 'suppress' means to subdue — neither fits 'replace guidelines'.

**Answer.** supersede.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct word: 'The blast had a serious ____ on the surrounding structures.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Effect' is the noun (a result); 'affect' is usually the verb (to influence).

**Working.** A noun is needed after 'a serious', so 'effect' is correct.

**Answer.** effect.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A scientist who studies the origin, composition and structure of rocks is a ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Field names derive from their subject roots: 'petro-' means rock.

**Working.** A petrologist studies rocks; a cartographer makes maps, a meteorologist studies weather, and a hydrologist studies water.

**Answer.** petrologist.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the option that best captures the meaning of 'EQUIVOCAL' in: 'The survey gave equivocal results.'
options: open to more than one interpretation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Equivocal' means ambiguous or capable of being understood in more than one way.

**Working.** Equivocal results are uncertain and admit multiple interpretations; the antonym would be 'unequivocal' (clear).

**Answer.** open to more than one interpretation.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Although the project was delayed, the team remained ____ about meeting the final deadline.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Although' sets up a contrast: despite a setback, a positive attitude persisted.

**Working.** The contrast with the delay calls for a positive word — 'optimistic'.

**Answer.** optimistic.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The auditor's ____ examination revealed discrepancies that others had overlooked.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Detecting overlooked discrepancies requires a very thorough, careful examination.

**Working.** 'Scrupulous' means diligent and exact; 'cursory' (hasty) would miss details.

**Answer.** scrupulous.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in both blanks: 'The geologist's hypothesis, though initially ____, was eventually ____ by overwhelming field evidence.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Though' signals a reversal between the two blanks.

**Working.** A hypothesis first rejected ('dismissed') but later proved right ('vindicated') gives the required contrast supported by 'overwhelming evidence'.

**Answer.** dismissed ... vindicated.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Despite the ____ conditions underground, the crew completed the survey without incident.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Despite' indicates that the work succeeded in spite of difficulty.

**Working.** Difficulty implies adverse conditions — 'hazardous'; the other words describe easy conditions.

**Answer.** hazardous.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The committee's decision was so ____ that not a single member raised an objection.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** No objections from anyone implies complete agreement.

**Working.** 'Unanimous' means fully agreed; the other options imply disagreement or hesitation.

**Answer.** unanimous.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The engineer's explanation was ____; everyone understood it immediately.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Immediate understanding implies clarity.

**Working.** 'Lucid' means clear and easy to understand; the other options mean unclear.

**Answer.** lucid.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'New safety measures were introduced to ____ the risk of roof collapse.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Safety measures aim to reduce risk.

**Working.** 'Mitigate' means to make less severe; 'aggravate' would worsen the risk.

**Answer.** mitigate.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in both blanks: 'Because the data were ____, the analysts could draw only ____ conclusions.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Because' links a cause (quality of data) to an effect (strength of conclusions).

**Working.** Poor ('incomplete') data justify only cautious ('tentative') conclusions; the other pairs imply strong data with strong conclusions, contradicting the limitation.

**Answer.** incomplete ... tentative.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: MINER : ORE :: FARMER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'worker : product extracted/produced'.

**Working.** A miner extracts ore; a farmer produces a crop. A tractor and field are tools/locations, not products.

**Answer.** crop.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: THERMOMETER : TEMPERATURE :: BAROMETER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'instrument : quantity it measures'.

**Working.** A thermometer measures temperature; a barometer measures atmospheric pressure.

**Answer.** pressure.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: DROUGHT : WATER :: FAMINE : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'crisis : the resource that is scarce'.

**Working.** A drought is a severe shortage of water; a famine is a severe shortage of food.

**Answer.** food.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: ORE : SMELTING :: WHEAT : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'raw material : the processing step that yields a usable product'.

**Working.** Smelting processes ore into metal; milling processes wheat into flour. Harvesting/sowing are field operations, not refining.

**Answer.** milling.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: SEISMOGRAPH : EARTHQUAKE :: ANEMOMETER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'instrument : phenomenon it records/measures'.

**Working.** A seismograph records earthquakes; an anemometer measures wind speed.

**Answer.** wind speed.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: PROFLIGATE : THRIFT :: GARRULOUS : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'trait : the quality it conspicuously lacks'.

**Working.** A profligate (wasteful) person lacks thrift; a garrulous (excessively talkative) person lacks reticence (reserve in speech).

**Answer.** reticence.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'Open-pit mining removes large volumes of overburden to expose ore near the surface. Although it is cheaper per tonne than underground mining, it disturbs far larger areas of land and demands extensive reclamation afterward.'

Which statement is best supported by the passage?
options: Open-pit mining is less expensive per tonne but has a greater surface footprint.
Underground mining always disturbs more land than open-pit mining.
Open-pit mining requires no land reclamation.
Open-pit mining is more expensive per tonne than underground mining.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Choose the option directly entailed by the text, not one that overstates it.

**Working.** The passage says open-pit is 'cheaper per tonne' yet 'disturbs far larger areas' — exactly option (a). The others contradict the passage.

**Answer.** Open-pit mining is less expensive per tonne but has a greater surface footprint.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'The introduction of automated haulage trucks reduced accidents in the pit by 40%. However, it also displaced a number of human drivers, prompting the company to launch a retraining programme.'

It can be inferred that the company:
options: sought to address the impact of automation on its workforce
abandoned automation because of the job losses
increased the number of human drivers it employed
ignored the workers displaced by automation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An inference must follow logically from stated facts.

**Working.** Launching a retraining programme in response to displaced drivers shows the company addressed automation's workforce impact.

**Answer.** sought to address the impact of automation on its workforce.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'Acid mine drainage occurs when sulphide minerals react with water and oxygen. The resulting acidic water can dissolve heavy metals, contaminating nearby streams long after a mine has closed.'

According to the passage, acid mine drainage:
options: can continue to pollute water even after mining stops
occurs only while a mine is operating
is caused by the complete absence of oxygen
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Match the option to the explicit wording of the passage.

**Working.** The text states contamination persists 'long after a mine has closed', so pollution can continue post-closure. Oxygen is required (not absent), and metals are dissolved (not neutralised).

**Answer.** can continue to pollute water even after mining stops.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'Critics argue that subsidies for fossil-fuel extraction distort energy markets and delay the transition to renewables. Proponents counter that such subsidies protect jobs and keep energy affordable in the short term.'

The passage primarily presents:
options: two opposing viewpoints on fossil-fuel subsidies
a definitive argument against renewable energy
a neutral historical account of energy policy
evidence proving subsidies have no effect
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Identify the rhetorical structure (argument, narrative, comparison of views).

**Working.** The passage states what 'critics argue' and what 'proponents counter', i.e., two opposing positions, without endorsing either.

**Answer.** two opposing viewpoints on fossil-fuel subsidies.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Arrange the sentences into a coherent paragraph.
P: Finally, the processed ore is transported to the smelter.
Q: First, the ore body is located through exploratory drilling.
R: Then the ore is extracted and hauled to the surface.
S: Next, it is crushed and concentrated at the mill.
The correct order is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order the steps by their explicit sequence markers (First, Then, Next, Finally).

**Working.** First locate (Q), then extract and haul (R), next crush/concentrate (S), finally transport to smelter (P).

**Answer.** Q, R, S, P.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Arrange the sentences into a coherent paragraph.
P: As a result, the roof was reinforced with additional bolts.
Q: During inspection, engineers noticed hairline cracks in the roof.
R: They immediately evacuated the section as a precaution.
S: A detailed survey then confirmed that the rock was unstable.
The correct order is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Arrange by cause-and-effect and chronological cues.

**Working.** Cracks are noticed (Q), the area is evacuated as a precaution (R), a survey confirms instability (S), and as a result bolts are added (P).

**Answer.** Q, R, S, P.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: What is $35\%$ of $240$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Percent means parts per hundred.

**Working.**
$$\frac{35}{100}\times 240 = 84$$

**Answer.** $84$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A number increased by $20\%$ becomes $90$. The original number is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Increasing by $20\%$ multiplies the number by $1.2$.

**Working.**
$$x\times 1.2 = 90 \Rightarrow x = \frac{90}{1.2} = 75$$

**Answer.** $75$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A price is first increased by $10\%$ and then decreased by $10\%$. The net percentage change in the price is _____ % (use a negative sign for a decrease).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combine successive factors multiplicatively.

**Working.**
$$1.10\times 0.90 = 0.99 \Rightarrow -1\%$$

**Answer.** $-1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In an examination $40\%$ marks are required to pass. A student who scores $178$ marks fails by $22$ marks. The maximum marks of the examination are _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pass mark = score + shortfall.

**Working.** Pass mark $=178+22=200=0.40\,M$, so
$$M=\frac{200}{0.40}=500$$

**Answer.** $500$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If $a:b = 2:3$ and $b:c = 4:5$, then $a:c$ equals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Make $b$ common by scaling the two ratios.

**Working.** Scale to $a:b:c = 8:12:15$, hence $a:c = 8:15$.

**Answer.** $8:15$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If $12$ workers build a wall in $10$ days, how many days will $15$ workers (of equal efficiency) take to build the same wall?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Workers and time are inversely proportional for a fixed task.

**Working.**
$$\text{days}=\frac{12\times 10}{15}=8$$

**Answer.** $8$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $10, 20, 30, 40$ and $50$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum divided by count.

**Working.**
$$\frac{10+20+30+40+50}{5}=\frac{150}{5}=30$$

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $5$ numbers is $18$. If one of the numbers, $22$, is removed, the average of the remaining four numbers is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Recover the total from the average, then adjust.

**Working.** Sum $=5\times 18=90$; remaining sum $=90-22=68$, so average $=\dfrac{68}{4}=17$.

**Answer.** $17$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average age of $30$ students is $12$ years. When the teacher's age is included, the average rises to $13$ years. The teacher's age is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total age = average times number of people.

**Working.** Students' total $=30\times 12=360$; with teacher $=31\times 13=403$. Teacher $=403-360=43$.

**Answer.** $43$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The simple interest on $5000$ at $8\%$ per annum for $3$ years is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{SI}=\dfrac{P\,r\,t}{100}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{5000\times 8\times 3}{100}=1200$$

**Answer.** $1200$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sum of money doubles itself in $8$ years under simple interest. The rate of interest per annum is _____ % .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Doubling means the interest equals the principal.

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{P\,r\,8}{100}\Rightarrow r=\dfrac{100}{8}=12.5$.

**Answer.** $12.5\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The compound interest on $8000$ at $10\%$ per annum, compounded annually for $2$ years, is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — CI vs SI Difference
questions.stem: The difference between the compound interest and the simple interest on $10000$ at $10\%$ per annum for $2$ years is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For $2$ years the difference is $P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$10000\times (0.1)^2 = 10000\times 0.01 = 100$$

**Answer.** $100$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article costing $200$ is sold for $250$. The profit percentage is _____ % .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit\% $=\dfrac{\text{SP}-\text{CP}}{\text{CP}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{250-200}{200}\times 100 = 25\%$$

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article sold for $480$ incurs a loss of $20\%$. Its cost price is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $20\%$ loss means SP $=0.8\times$ CP.

**Working.**
$$\text{CP}=\frac{480}{0.8}=600$$

**Answer.** $600$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An item is marked at $1200$ and offered with two successive discounts of $10\%$ and $20\%$. The final selling price is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Successive discounts multiply the surviving fractions.

**Working.**
$$1200\times 0.90\times 0.80 = 864$$

**Answer.** $864$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: By selling $20$ articles, a trader gains the cost price of $5$ articles. The profit percentage is _____ % .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit\% = (gain in cost units) / (cost units sold) $\times 100$.

**Working.** Gain $=5\,\text{CP}$ on a cost of $20\,\text{CP}$, so $\dfrac{5}{20}\times 100 = 25\%$.

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can finish a job in $12$ days and B in $6$ days. Working together, they finish it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the per-day work rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{6}=\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{2}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ days.

**Answer.** $4$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can complete a task in $10$ days and B in $15$ days. Working together, they complete it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combined rate is the sum of individual rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{10}+\dfrac{1}{15}=\dfrac{3+2}{30}=\dfrac{5}{30}=\dfrac{1}{6}$, so $6$ days.

**Answer.** $6$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is twice as efficient as B, and together they finish a job in $12$ days. A alone would take
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Let B's rate be $x$; then A's rate is $2x$.

**Working.** $3x=\dfrac{1}{12}\Rightarrow x=\dfrac{1}{36}$; A's rate $=2x=\dfrac{1}{18}$, so A alone takes $18$ days.

**Answer.** $18$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — Time, Speed and Distance
questions.stem: A car travels $180$ km in $3$ hours. Its average speed is _____ km/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Speed = distance / time.

**Working.** $\dfrac{180}{3}=60$ km/h.

**Answer.** $60$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert $72$ km/h into m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply km/h by $\tfrac{5}{18}$ to get m/s.

**Working.** $72\times\dfrac{5}{18}=20$ m/s.

**Answer.** $20$ m/s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A train $150$ m long moving at $54$ km/h crosses a stationary pole in _____ seconds.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** To cross a pole, the train covers its own length.

**Working.** $54$ km/h $=54\times\tfrac{5}{18}=15$ m/s; time $=\dfrac{150}{15}=10$ s.

**Answer.** $10$ s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A train $200$ m long moving at $36$ km/h crosses a platform $100$ m long in _____ seconds.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** To cross a platform, the train covers (its length + platform length).

**Working.** Speed $=36\times\tfrac{5}{18}=10$ m/s; distance $=200+100=300$ m; time $=\dfrac{300}{10}=30$ s.

**Answer.** $30$ s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A boat's speed in still water is $10$ km/h and the stream flows at $2$ km/h. The time taken to travel $24$ km downstream is _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed = boat + stream.

**Working.** Downstream speed $=10+2=12$ km/h; time $=\dfrac{24}{12}=2$ h.

**Answer.** $2$ h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A boat covers $16$ km downstream in $2$ hours and returns the same distance upstream in $4$ hours. The speed of the boat in still water is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Boat speed = (downstream + upstream)/2.

**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{16}{2}=8$, upstream $=\tfrac{16}{4}=4$ km/h; boat $=\dfrac{8+4}{2}=6$ km/h.

**Answer.** $6$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A man travels to a town at $40$ km/h and returns along the same road at $60$ km/h. His average speed for the whole journey is _____ km/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For equal distances, average speed is the harmonic mean.

**Working.**
$$\frac{2\times 40\times 60}{40+60}=\frac{4800}{100}=48$$

**Answer.** $48$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in $6$ hours and pipe B in $12$ hours. With both open, the tank fills in _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the filling rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{6}+\dfrac{1}{12}=\dfrac{2+1}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ hours.

**Answer.** $4$ hours.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in $10$ hours while pipe B empties it in $15$ hours. With both open, the tank fills in
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Subtract the emptying rate from the filling rate.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{10}-\dfrac{1}{15}=\dfrac{3-2}{30}=\dfrac{1}{30}$, so $30$ hours.

**Answer.** $30$ hours.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A $40$-litre mixture contains milk and water in the ratio $3:1$. The volume of water in it is _____ litres.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Water fraction $=\dfrac{1}{3+1}$.

**Working.** $40\times\dfrac{1}{4}=10$ litres.

**Answer.** $10$ litres.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In what ratio should rice costing $20$ per kg be mixed with rice costing $30$ per kg so that the mixture costs $24$ per kg?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Alligation: ratio $=$ (dearer $-$ mean) : (mean $-$ cheaper).

**Working.** $(30-24):(24-20)=6:4=3:2$ (cheaper : dearer).

**Answer.** $3:2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A invests $5000$ and B invests $7000$ in a business for the same period. A profit of $6000$ is shared in the ratio of their investments. B's share is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit splits in the ratio of capital (equal time).

**Working.** Ratio $=5:7$; B's share $=6000\times\dfrac{7}{12}=3500$.

**Answer.** $3500$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A invests $12000$ for $6$ months and B invests $9000$ for $8$ months. Their profit-sharing ratio A : B is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share is proportional to (capital $\times$ time).

**Working.** A $=12000\times 6=72000$; B $=9000\times 8=72000$; ratio $=1:1$.

**Answer.** $1:1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is three times as old as B, and the sum of their ages is $48$ years. A's age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express both ages in one variable.

**Working.** $A=3B$ and $A+B=48\Rightarrow 4B=48\Rightarrow B=12,\;A=36$.

**Answer.** $36$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Five years ago, a man was seven times as old as his son. The man is now $40$ years old. The son's present age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Translate the past condition into an equation.

**Working.** Five years ago the man was $35$, so $35=7(\text{son}-5)\Rightarrow \text{son}-5=5\Rightarrow \text{son}=10$.

**Answer.** $10$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The highest common factor (HCF) of $24$ and $36$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** HCF is the product of common prime factors at lowest powers.

**Working.** $24=2^3\cdot 3$, $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$; HCF $=2^2\cdot 3=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The least common multiple (LCM) of $6$ and $8$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** LCM is the product of all prime factors at highest powers.

**Working.** $6=2\cdot 3$, $8=2^3$; LCM $=2^3\cdot 3=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The largest number that divides $70$ and $125$, leaving remainders $5$ and $8$ respectively, is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Subtract the remainders, then take the HCF.

**Working.** $70-5=65$, $125-8=117$; HCF$(65,117)=13$.

**Answer.** $13$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The smallest positive number that is exactly divisible by $12$, $15$ and $20$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The smallest common multiple is the LCM.

**Working.** $12=2^2\cdot3$, $15=3\cdot5$, $20=2^2\cdot5$; LCM $=2^2\cdot3\cdot5=60$.

**Answer.** $60$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which of the following numbers is divisible by $9$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A number is divisible by $9$ if its digit sum is divisible by $9$.

**Working.** $1+2+3+4+8=18$, which is divisible by $9$.

**Answer.** $12348$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The number of distinct ways to arrange all the letters of the word 'MINE' is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** With all letters distinct, arrangements $=n!$.

**Working.** $4!=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The number of ways to choose $2$ items from $6$ distinct items is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order does not matter, so use $\binom{n}{r}$.

**Working.** $\binom{6}{2}=\dfrac{6\times5}{2}=15$.

**Answer.** $15$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: From $4$ engineers and $3$ geologists, a committee of $3$ is to be formed with exactly $2$ engineers. The number of ways is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply independent choices.

**Working.** $\binom{4}{2}\times\binom{3}{1}=6\times3=18$.

**Answer.** $18$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The number of distinct arrangements of all the letters of the word 'LEVEL' is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Divide $n!$ by the factorials of repeated-letter counts.

**Working.** 'LEVEL' has L$\times2$, E$\times2$, V$\times1$: $\dfrac{5!}{2!\,2!}=\dfrac{120}{4}=30$.

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The probability of getting a head in a single toss of a fair coin is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Probability = favourable / total equally likely outcomes.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{2}=0.5$.

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two fair dice are rolled. The probability that their sum is $8$ is _____ (round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count favourable pairs out of $36$.

**Working.** Sum $8$: $(2,6),(3,5),(4,4),(5,3),(6,2)=5$ ways; $\dfrac{5}{36}\approx 0.14$.

**Answer.** $\approx 0.14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A bag contains $3$ red and $2$ blue balls. The probability of drawing a red ball at random is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Probability = favourable / total.

**Working.** $\dfrac{3}{3+2}=\dfrac{3}{5}$.

**Answer.** $\dfrac{3}{5}$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A card is drawn at random from a standard $52$-card deck. The probability that it is an ace is _____ (round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** There are $4$ aces in $52$ cards.

**Working.** $\dfrac{4}{52}=\dfrac{1}{13}\approx 0.08$.

**Answer.** $\approx 0.08$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A bag has $4$ white and $6$ black balls. Two balls are drawn without replacement. The probability that both are white is _____ (round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply conditional probabilities for draws without replacement.

**Working.** $\dfrac{4}{10}\times\dfrac{3}{9}=\dfrac{12}{90}=\dfrac{2}{15}\approx 0.13$.

**Answer.** $\approx 0.13$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A town's population of $8000$ grows by $5\%$ each year. After $2$ years the population is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Compound growth multiplies by $(1.05)$ each year.

**Working.** $8000\times(1.05)^2=8000\times1.1025=8820$.

**Answer.** $8820$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $5000$ is divided among A, B and C in the ratio $2:3:5$. C's share is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share = total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** C's fraction $=\dfrac{5}{2+3+5}=\dfrac{5}{10}$, so $5000\times\dfrac{1}{2}=2500$.

**Answer.** $2500$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of the first $10$ natural numbers is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average of first $n$ natural numbers $=\dfrac{n+1}{2}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{10+1}{2}=5.5$.

**Answer.** $5.5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article marked $25\%$ above its cost price is sold at a $10\%$ discount. The profit percentage is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply markup then discount on a base cost of $100$.

**Working.** CP $=100$, MP $=125$, SP $=125\times0.9=112.5$; profit $=12.5\%$.

**Answer.** $12.5\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — Time, Speed and Distance
questions.stem: Two trains $60$ km apart move toward each other at $40$ km/h and $20$ km/h. The time taken to meet is _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Closing speed = sum of speeds when moving toward each other.

**Working.** Relative speed $=40+20=60$ km/h; time $=\dfrac{60}{60}=1$ h.

**Answer.** $1$ hour.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle of length $12$ and width $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area of rectangle $=\text{length}\times\text{width}$.

**Working.** $12\times 5 = 60$.

**Answer.** $60$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The perimeter of a square of side $9$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perimeter of a square $=4\times\text{side}$.

**Working.** $4\times 9 = 36$.

**Answer.** $36$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a circle of radius $7$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\pi r^2$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{22}{7}\times 7^2 = 22\times 7 = 154$.

**Answer.** $154$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a triangle with base $10$ and height $6$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\tfrac{1}{2}\,\text{base}\times\text{height}$.

**Working.** $\tfrac{1}{2}\times 10\times 6 = 30$.

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The volume of a cube of side $4$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume of a cube $=\text{side}^3$.

**Working.** $4^3=64$.

**Answer.** $64$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The volume of a cylinder of radius $7$ and height $10$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume $=\pi r^2 h$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{22}{7}\times 49\times 10 = 22\times 7\times 10 = 1540$.

**Answer.** $1540$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The volume of a cone of radius $3$ and height $7$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume of a cone $=\tfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.

**Working.** $\tfrac{1}{3}\times\dfrac{22}{7}\times 9\times 7 = \tfrac{1}{3}\times 22\times 9 = 66$.

**Answer.** $66$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The volume of a sphere of radius $3$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$, round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume of a sphere $=\tfrac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.

**Working.** $\tfrac{4}{3}\times\dfrac{22}{7}\times 27 = \dfrac{2376}{21}\approx 113.14$.

**Answer.** $\approx 113.14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the right triangle shown, the legs are $3$ and $4$. The length of the hypotenuse is _____ .

figure.caption: Right triangle with legs 3 and 4 and the right angle at the bottom-left.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='40,130 160,130 40,40' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='118' width='12' height='12' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1'/><text x='95' y='145' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>4</text><text x='20' y='90' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>3</text><text x='105' y='80' font-size='12' fill='#ef4444'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pythagoras: hypotenuse $=\sqrt{a^2+b^2}$.

**Working.** $\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=\sqrt{25}=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio $1:2:3$. The largest angle is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angles of a triangle sum to $180^\circ$.

**Working.** Parts $=1+2+3=6$; largest $=\dfrac{3}{6}\times 180 = 90^\circ$.

**Answer.** $90$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one interior angle is $70^\circ$ (see figure), its co-interior (same-side) angle is

figure.caption: Two parallel lines crossed by a transversal; one interior angle is marked 70 degrees.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='200' y2='60' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='200' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='30' x2='160' y2='150' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><text x='95' y='78' font-size='11' fill='#ef4444'>70</text><text x='118' y='112' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Co-interior (same-side interior) angles between parallel lines are supplementary.

**Working.** $180^\circ-70^\circ=110^\circ$.

**Answer.** $110^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the right triangle shown, the hypotenuse is $13$ and one leg is $5$. The other leg is _____ .

figure.caption: Right triangle with hypotenuse 13, one leg 5, and the other leg unknown.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='40,140 170,140 40,40' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.10)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='128' width='12' height='12' fill='none' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1'/><text x='100' y='155' font-size='12' fill='#ef4444'>?</text><text x='20' y='95' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>5</text><text x='112' y='85' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>13</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pythagoras: leg $=\sqrt{c^2-a^2}$.

**Working.** $\sqrt{13^2-5^2}=\sqrt{169-25}=\sqrt{144}=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two triangles are similar with corresponding sides in the ratio $2:3$. The ratio of their areas is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Areas of similar figures scale as the square of the side ratio.

**Working.** $(2:3)^2 = 4:9$.

**Answer.** $4:9$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A wire of length $44$ cm is bent to form a circle (use $\pi=22/7$). The radius of the circle is _____ cm.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The wire length equals the circumference $2\pi r$.

**Working.** $2\times\dfrac{22}{7}\times r = 44\Rightarrow r = \dfrac{44\times 7}{44}=7$.

**Answer.** $7$ cm.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The length of the diagonal of a rectangle of sides $8$ and $6$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The diagonal is the hypotenuse of a right triangle with the two sides as legs.

**Working.** $\sqrt{8^2+6^2}=\sqrt{100}=10$.

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows annual sales (in units) for four years: $40, 60, 30, 80$. The highest annual sales figure is _____ units.

figure.caption: Bar chart of annual sales for 2018-2021: 40, 60, 30, 80 units.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='40' y='100' width='28' height='60' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='80' y='70' width='28' height='90' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='120' y='115' width='28' height='45' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='160' y='40' width='28' height='120' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='44' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2018</text><text x='84' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2019</text><text x='124' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2020</text><text x='164' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2021</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Read the tallest bar.

**Working.** The values are $40,60,30,80$; the maximum is $80$ (year 2021).

**Answer.** $80$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: From the same sales data ($40, 60, 30, 80$ units over four years), the average annual sales is _____ units.

figure.caption: Bar chart of annual sales for 2018-2021: 40, 60, 30, 80 units.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='40' y='100' width='28' height='60' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='80' y='70' width='28' height='90' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='120' y='115' width='28' height='45' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='160' y='40' width='28' height='120' fill='#6366f1'/><line x1='30' y1='82.5' x2='220' y2='82.5' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='5 3'/><text x='150' y='78' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>average</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = total / number of years.

**Working.** $\dfrac{40+60+30+80}{4}=\dfrac{210}{4}=52.5$.

**Answer.** $52.5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A monthly budget of $3600$ is split as Labour $25\%$, Material $40\%$, Equipment $20\%$ and Other $15\%$ (see pie chart). The amount spent on Labour is

figure.caption: Pie chart of a 3600 budget: Labour 25%, Material 40%, Equipment 20%, Other 15%.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 230 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='110' cy='90' r='70' fill='none' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='180' y2='90' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='110' y2='20' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='69' y2='147' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='151' y2='147' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><text x='130' y='60' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>Labour 25%</text><text x='40' y='70' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>Material 40%</text><text x='70' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#f59e0b'>Equip 20%</text><text x='150' y='130' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>Other 15%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sector amount = percentage of the total.

**Working.** $25\%$ of $3600 = 0.25\times 3600 = 900$.

**Answer.** $900$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The line graph shows monthly production (in tonnes): Jan $50$, Feb $55$, Mar $60$, Apr $65$. The percentage increase from January to April is _____ % .

figure.caption: Line graph of monthly production rising from 50 (Jan) to 65 (Apr).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='30' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='40,140 100,120 160,100 200,80' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='40' cy='140' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='100' cy='120' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='160' cy='100' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='200' cy='80' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='34' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>Jan</text><text x='192' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>Apr</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Percentage increase $=\dfrac{\text{new}-\text{old}}{\text{old}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{65-50}{50}\times100 = 30\%$.

**Answer.** $30\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The grouped bars compare products X and Y over three years — X: $20, 30, 40$; Y: $25, 28, 30$. The year in which the difference between X and Y is greatest is

figure.caption: Grouped bar chart comparing products X (blue) and Y (green) across three years.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='45' y='120' width='18' height='40' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='65' y='110' width='18' height='50' fill='#10b981'/><rect x='110' y='100' width='18' height='60' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='130' y='104' width='18' height='56' fill='#10b981'/><rect x='175' y='80' width='18' height='80' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='195' y='100' width='18' height='60' fill='#10b981'/><text x='52' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2018</text><text x='117' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2019</text><text x='182' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2020</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Compute the absolute difference for each year.

**Working.** Differences: $|20-25|=5$, $|30-28|=2$, $|40-30|=10$. The greatest is in 2020.

**Answer.** $2020$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A company's headcount by department is A $30$, B $50$, C $20$ (see bar chart). Department B's share of the total workforce is _____ % .

figure.caption: Bar chart of department headcounts: A 30, B 50, C 20.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='200' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='50' y='100' width='30' height='60' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='100' y='60' width='30' height='100' fill='#10b981'/><rect x='150' y='120' width='30' height='40' fill='#f59e0b'/><text x='60' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>A</text><text x='110' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>B</text><text x='160' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>C</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share $=\dfrac{\text{part}}{\text{total}}\times100$.

**Working.** Total $=30+50+20=100$; B's share $=\dfrac{50}{100}\times100 = 50\%$.

**Answer.** $50\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The histogram of test scores has class frequencies $4, 8, 10, 6, 2$. The frequency of the modal class is _____ .

figure.caption: Histogram with adjacent bars of frequencies 4, 8, 10, 6, 2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='40' y='120' width='32' height='40' fill='#6366f1' stroke='#fff'/><rect x='72' y='80' width='32' height='80' fill='#6366f1' stroke='#fff'/><rect x='104' y='60' width='32' height='100' fill='#10b981' stroke='#fff'/><rect x='136' y='100' width='32' height='60' fill='#6366f1' stroke='#fff'/><rect x='168' y='140' width='32' height='20' fill='#6366f1' stroke='#fff'/><text x='112' y='54' font-size='9' fill='#10b981'>modal</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The modal class is the one with the highest frequency.

**Working.** The frequencies are $4,8,10,6,2$; the maximum is $10$.

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Monthly rainfall (in mm) over four months is $10, 20, 30, 40$ (see chart). The total rainfall over the four months is _____ mm.

figure.caption: Bar chart of monthly rainfall: 10, 20, 30, 40 mm.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='45' y='135' width='28' height='25' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='90' y='110' width='28' height='50' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='135' y='85' width='28' height='75' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='180' y='60' width='28' height='100' fill='#0ea5e9'/><text x='52' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>M1</text><text x='187' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>M4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total = sum of monthly values.

**Working.** $10+20+30+40 = 100$ mm.

**Answer.** $100$ mm.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term of the series: $2, 6, 12, 20, 30, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Look at the first differences.

**Working.** Differences are $4,6,8,10,\dots$ (increasing by $2$); next difference $=12$, so $30+12=42$.

**Answer.** $42$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $2, 4, 8, 16, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each term is double the previous (geometric, ratio $2$).

**Working.** $16\times 2 = 32$.

**Answer.** $32$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $1, 4, 9, 16, 25, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The terms are perfect squares $n^2$.

**Working.** Next is $6^2 = 36$.

**Answer.** $36$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $3, 7, 15, 31, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each term follows $a_{n+1}=2a_n+1$.

**Working.** $31\times 2 + 1 = 63$.

**Answer.** $63$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fibonacci: each term is the sum of the two preceding terms.

**Working.** $5+8 = 13$.

**Answer.** $13$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $5, 11, 23, 47, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each term follows $a_{n+1}=2a_n+1$.

**Working.** $47\times 2 + 1 = 95$.

**Answer.** $95$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next letter in the series: A, C, E, G, ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The letters advance by two positions each time (odd-positioned letters).

**Working.** After G (7th letter), skip H to reach I.

**Answer.** I.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The sum of the first $20$ natural numbers is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum $=\dfrac{n(n+1)}{2}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{20\times 21}{2}=210$.

**Answer.** $210$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express the right side as a power of the same base.

**Working.** $32=2^5$, so $x=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The units digit of $7^{4}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Units digits of powers of $7$ cycle $7, 9, 3, 1$ with period $4$.

**Working.** $7^4$ ends the cycle at $1$ (indeed $7^4=2401$).

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The remainder when $2^{10}$ is divided by $7$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Use $2^3=8\equiv 1 \pmod 7$.

**Working.** $2^{10}=(2^3)^3\cdot 2 \equiv 1^3\cdot 2 = 2 \pmod 7$.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The total surface area of a cube of side $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Surface area of a cube $=6\,(\text{side})^2$.

**Working.** $6\times 5^2 = 6\times 25 = 150$.

**Answer.** $150$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The measure of each interior angle of a regular hexagon is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Interior angle $=\dfrac{(n-2)\times 180}{n}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{(6-2)\times 180}{6}=\dfrac{720}{6}=120^\circ$.

**Answer.** $120$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Expressed as a percentage, $0.25$ equals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply a decimal by $100$ to convert to a percentage.

**Working.** $0.25\times 100 = 25\%$.

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scale the ratio by the same factor.

**Working.** $x=12=3\times 4$, so $y=4\times 4 = 16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The number of positive divisors of $36$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For $N=p^a q^b$, the divisor count is $(a+1)(b+1)$.

**Working.** $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$, so $(2+1)(2+1)=9$.

**Answer.** $9$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: All miners are workers. All workers are humans. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Transitivity of universal affirmatives.

**Working.** Miners ⊆ workers ⊆ humans, so all miners are humans.

**Answer.** All miners are humans.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Some books are pens. All pens are red. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combine the particular and universal premises.

**Working.** The books that are pens must be red (all pens are red), so some books are red.

**Answer.** Some books are red.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: All cats are animals. Some animals are wild. Which conclusion is valid?
options: No definite conclusion about cats being wild can be drawn
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A particular premise about the broader set need not apply to the subset.

**Working.** 'Some animals are wild' does not specify whether any are cats, so nothing definite about wild cats follows.

**Answer.** No definite conclusion can be drawn.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: No metals are gases. All mercury is metal. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combine a universal negative with a universal affirmative.

**Working.** Mercury ⊆ metals and metals ∩ gases $=\varnothing$, so no mercury is a gas.

**Answer.** No mercury is gas.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Given 'All P are Q' and 'All Q are R' (with P non-empty), which of the following necessarily follow?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply transitivity and existential import of a non-empty set.

**Working.** P ⊆ Q ⊆ R gives 'All P are R'; since P is non-empty, 'Some R are P' holds; 'All Q are R' gives 'Some Q are R'. 'All R are P' is not implied (R may be larger).

**Answer.** All P are R; Some R are P; Some Q are R.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve the description inside-out.

**Working.** 'The only daughter of my mother' is the woman herself, so 'his mother' is the woman — she is his mother.

**Answer.** Mother.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is B's brother. C is A's mother. D is C's father. How is B related to D?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Build the family tree level by level.

**Working.** C is the mother of A and B; D is C's father, hence the grandparent of A and B. So B is D's grandchild.

**Answer.** Grandchild.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is she related to the man?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve the chain from the innermost phrase.

**Working.** 'My grandfather's only son' is the man's father; his daughter is the man's sister.

**Answer.** Sister.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: P is the father of Q. Q is the sister of R. S is the mother of R. How is P related to S?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Identify shared children to fix the spousal link.

**Working.** P fathers Q and R; S mothers R (and Q). Sharing the same children, P and S are spouses, so P is the husband of S.

**Answer.** Husband.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: X is the brother of the son of Y's son. How is X related to Y?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve generations from the inside out.

**Working.** 'The son of Y's son' is Y's grandson; X is his brother, hence also Y's grandson.

**Answer.** Grandson.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A man walks $3$ km north and then $4$ km east. His straight-line distance from the starting point is _____ km.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perpendicular legs form a right triangle; use Pythagoras.

**Working.** $\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=\sqrt{25}=5$.

**Answer.** $5$ km.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A person faces north, turns $90^\circ$ clockwise, then turns $180^\circ$. Which direction is the person now facing?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Track each turn from the current facing.

**Working.** North → ($90^\circ$ CW) East → ($180^\circ$) West.

**Answer.** West.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A person walks $6$ km south and then $8$ km west. The straight-line distance from the start is _____ km.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perpendicular displacements; apply Pythagoras.

**Working.** $\sqrt{6^2+8^2}=\sqrt{100}=10$.

**Answer.** $10$ km.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: At sunrise, the shadow of a vertical pole falls toward which direction?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A shadow points away from the sun.

**Working.** At sunrise the sun is in the east, so the shadow falls toward the west.

**Answer.** West.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A man walks $10$ m north, then $10$ m east, then $10$ m south. His final position relative to the start is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cancel opposite displacements.

**Working.** North $10$ and south $10$ cancel; the net displacement is $10$ m east.

**Answer.** $10$ m east.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If CAT is coded as DBU, then DOG is coded as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each letter shifts forward by one position.

**Working.** D→E, O→P, G→H gives EPH.

**Answer.** EPH.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If 'MINE' is coded as 'NJOF', then 'COAL' is coded as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each letter is shifted forward by one (the +1 cipher).

**Working.** C→D, O→P, A→B, L→M gives DPBM.

**Answer.** DPBM.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a code, 'ROCK' is written as 'QNBJ'. Using the same rule, 'SAND' is written as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Determine the shift: R→Q is $-1$.

**Working.** Each letter shifts back by one: S→R, A→Z, N→M, D→C gives RZMC.

**Answer.** RZMC.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Using A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26, the sum of the letter values of the word 'ORE' is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Replace each letter with its position value and add.

**Working.** O$=15$, R$=18$, E$=5$; sum $=38$.

**Answer.** $38$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Using A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26, the sum of the letter values of the word 'COAL' is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add positional values of each letter.

**Working.** C$=3$, O$=15$, A$=1$, L$=12$; sum $=31$.

**Answer.** $31$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a row of $20$ students, A is $7$th from the left. A's position from the right is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Position from right $=$ total $-$ position from left $+1$.

**Working.** $20-7+1=14$.

**Answer.** 14th.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a class, B is $5$th from the top and $12$th from the bottom in marks. The total number of students is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total $=$ rank from top $+$ rank from bottom $-1$.

**Working.** $5+12-1=16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a row, C is $9$th from the left and $11$th from the right. How many people are in the row?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total $=$ left rank $+$ right rank $-1$ (C counted once).

**Working.** $9+11-1=19$.

**Answer.** $19$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Five people are ranked by height: P is taller than Q, Q is taller than R, S is shorter than R, and T is the tallest. Who is the shortest?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Chain the inequalities into one order.

**Working.** $T>P>Q>R>S$, so S is the shortest.

**Answer.** S.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a queue, a person is $15$th from the front and $10$th from the back. The total number of people in the queue is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total $=$ front rank $+$ back rank $-1$.

**Working.** $15+10-1=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Statement: 'All safety helmets in the store are yellow.' Conclusion: 'No safety helmet in the store is blue.' Does the conclusion follow?
options: No, the conclusion does not follow
The conclusion is the opposite of the statement
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A thing of one exclusive colour cannot be another colour.

**Working.** If every helmet is yellow, none can be blue; the conclusion follows.

**Answer.** Yes, the conclusion follows.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If it rains, the match is cancelled. The match was not cancelled. Therefore,
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Modus tollens: deny the consequent to deny the antecedent.

**Working.** From 'rain → cancelled' and 'not cancelled', conclude 'not rain'.

**Answer.** It did not rain.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: All squares are rectangles. A given figure is not a rectangle. Therefore the figure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Contrapositive of 'square → rectangle' is 'not rectangle → not square'.

**Working.** Since it is not a rectangle, it cannot be a square.

**Answer.** is not a square.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Notice: 'During a fire, use the stairs; do not use the lift.' The assumption underlying this notice is that
options: lifts may malfunction or be unsafe during a fire
there is never a real fire
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An assumption is what must be taken for granted for the advice to make sense.

**Working.** Advising against lifts during a fire presumes lifts are unsafe then.

**Answer.** lifts may malfunction or be unsafe during a fire.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A mine increased its ventilation rate, and the measured methane concentration dropped. The most reasonable inference is that
options: better ventilation reduced the gas concentration
ventilation has no effect on gas
the gas caused the ventilation to increase
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Choose the inference best supported by a plausible mechanism.

**Working.** Increased airflow dilutes and removes methane, lowering its concentration.

**Answer.** better ventilation reduced the gas concentration.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Problem: frequent equipment breakdowns are delaying production. The most appropriate course of action is to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A good course of action addresses the root cause.

**Working.** Preventive maintenance reduces breakdowns and therefore the delays.

**Answer.** implement a preventive maintenance schedule.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at exactly $3{:}00$ is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hour mark spans $30^\circ$.

**Working.** At $3{:}00$ the hands are $3$ hour-marks apart: $3\times 30^\circ = 90^\circ$.

**Answer.** $90$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at exactly $4{:}00$ is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angle $=30^\circ\times$ (hours between hands).

**Working.** At $4{:}00$ the hands are $4$ marks apart: $4\times 30^\circ = 120^\circ$.

**Answer.** $120$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If today is Monday, what day of the week will it be after $15$ days?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Days repeat every $7$; take the remainder.

**Working.** $15\bmod 7 = 1$, so one day after Monday is Tuesday.

**Answer.** Tuesday.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a non-leap year, $1$ January is a Wednesday. What day is $1$ February?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** January has $31$ days; advance by $31\bmod 7$.

**Working.** $31\bmod 7 = 3$, so Wednesday $+3 =$ Saturday.

**Answer.** Saturday.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which arrangement best represents the relationship among Dog, Cat and Animal?
options: Two separate circles (Dog and Cat) lying inside one larger circle (Animal)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dogs and cats are disjoint subclasses of animals.

**Working.** Dog and Cat are non-overlapping sets, both contained within Animal.

**Answer.** Two separate circles inside a larger Animal circle.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a group of $50$ people, $30$ like tea, $25$ like coffee and $10$ like both. The number who like neither is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Inclusion-exclusion, then subtract from the total.

**Working.** Tea or coffee $=30+25-10=45$; neither $=50-45=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a class of $40$, $22$ play cricket, $18$ play football and $6$ play both. The number who play only cricket is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Only cricket $=$ cricket $-$ both.

**Working.** $22-6=16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The relationship among Doctors, Women and Engineers (each pair can share members) is best shown by
options: two overlapping circles and one separate
questions.solution:
**Concept.** When all three categories can share members, all pairs overlap.

**Working.** A person can be a woman, a doctor and/or an engineer, so all three circles intersect.

**Answer.** three mutually overlapping circles.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: To find a man's age, consider: (I) He is $5$ years older than his brother. (II) His brother is $20$ years old. Which is sufficient?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Identify the minimal information that fixes a unique value.

**Working.** I gives a relation, II gives the brother's age; together the man is $25$. Neither alone suffices.

**Answer.** Both I and II together.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Question: Is $x>10$? (I) $x$ is a positive even number. (II) $x=12$. Which is sufficient?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A statement is sufficient if it answers the question definitively.

**Working.** I allows $x=2$ (no) or $x=12$ (yes), so it is inconclusive; II fixes $x=12>10$.

**Answer.** II alone is sufficient.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Six people sit equally spaced around a circular table. If A sits directly opposite D, the number of seats between A and D (counting the shorter way) is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Directly opposite means half the table away.

**Working.** With $6$ seats, opposite seats have $\tfrac{6}{2}-1=2$ seats between them on each side.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If $5$ machines make $5$ widgets in $5$ minutes, how many minutes do $100$ machines take to make $100$ widgets?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the per-machine rate first.

**Working.** Each machine makes $1$ widget in $5$ minutes; $100$ machines make $100$ widgets in the same $5$ minutes.

**Answer.** $5$ minutes.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A clock loses $5$ minutes every hour. After $12$ hours of running, it is _____ minutes behind the correct time.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total loss $=$ rate of loss $\times$ time.

**Working.** $5\,\text{min/h}\times 12\,\text{h}=60$ minutes.

**Answer.** $60$ minutes.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the odd one out: Coal, Petroleum, Natural gas, Granite.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Group by a common property and spot the exception.

**Working.** Coal, petroleum and natural gas are fossil fuels; granite is an igneous rock, not a fuel.

**Answer.** Granite.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: When the letter shown is viewed in a vertical mirror placed to its right, the mirror image looks like the letter

figure.caption: The lowercase letter b with a dashed vertical mirror line on its right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='45' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>b</text><line x1='130' y1='12' x2='130' y2='108' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A vertical mirror swaps left and right.

**Working.** The bowl of 'b' is on the right; reflecting it to the left turns the shape into 'd'.

**Answer.** d.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A letter with a vertical line of symmetry looks unchanged in a vertical mirror. Which letter below has a vertical line of symmetry?

figure.caption: The capital letter A with a dashed vertical centre line.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='50' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>A</text><line x1='80' y1='12' x2='80' y2='108' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A vertical axis of symmetry leaves the letter unchanged under a vertical mirror.

**Working.** 'A' is symmetric about its central vertical line; F, P and Q are not.

**Answer.** A.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A wall clock seen in a mirror appears to read $4{:}00$. The actual time is

figure.caption: A clock face as seen in a mirror, appearing to read 4 o'clock.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='55' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='70' y2='30' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='105' y2='90' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><text x='62' y='20' font-size='11' fill='#64748b'>12</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Actual time $=12{:}00$ minus the mirror reading.

**Working.** $12{:}00-4{:}00=8{:}00$.

**Answer.** $8{:}00$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A water image is a reflection about a horizontal line. Which digit looks the same as its water image?

figure.caption: The digit 8 with a dashed horizontal reflection line through its middle.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='50' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>8</text><line x1='15' y1='60' x2='125' y2='60' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A digit unchanged by a horizontal flip has a horizontal axis of symmetry.

**Working.** '8' is symmetric top-to-bottom, so its water image is identical; 2, 5 and 7 are not.

**Answer.** 8.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which word reads the same when viewed in a vertical mirror?

figure.caption: The word MOM with a dashed vertical mirror line to its right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='30' y='72' font-size='52' fill='#334155'>MOM</text><line x1='195' y1='12' x2='195' y2='98' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each letter must be vertically symmetric and the word a palindrome.

**Working.** M, O, M are all vertically symmetric and 'MOM' is a palindrome, so the mirror image is identical.

**Answer.** MOM.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An arrow points to the north-east. In a vertical mirror, the arrow appears to point toward the

figure.caption: An arrow pointing to the north-east beside a dashed vertical mirror line.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='95' x2='95' y2='35' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='95,35 80,38 90,50' fill='#6366f1'/><line x1='140' y1='12' x2='140' y2='108' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A vertical mirror reverses the east-west component while keeping north-south.

**Working.** North-east (up and to the right) becomes up and to the left, i.e. north-west.

**Answer.** North-West.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded once in half and a single hole is punched through both layers. When unfolded, the number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded once in half with one hole punched through the folded sheet.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='70' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='90' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='50' cy='55' r='6' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each fold layer carries one hole.

**Working.** One fold gives $2$ layers; one punch makes $1\times 2 = 2$ holes.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded twice (into quarters) and one hole is punched through all layers. When unfolded, the number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded twice into a quarter with one hole punched.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='25' y='25' width='55' height='55' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.10)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='52' cy='52' r='6' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two folds create $2\times 2 = 4$ layers.

**Working.** One punch through $4$ layers gives $4$ holes.

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded twice (into quarters) and two holes are punched through all layers. When unfolded, the total number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded into quarters with two holes punched.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='25' y='25' width='55' height='55' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.10)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='42' cy='45' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='62' cy='62' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Holes $=$ punches $\times$ layers.

**Working.** $2$ folds give $4$ layers; $2$ punches make $2\times 4 = 8$ holes.

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded once in half and three holes are punched through both layers. When unfolded, the total number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded in half with three holes punched.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='70' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='90' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='45' cy='38' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='45' cy='55' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='45' cy='72' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Holes $=$ punches $\times$ layers.

**Working.** $1$ fold gives $2$ layers; $3$ punches make $3\times 2 = 6$ holes.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded three times (into one-eighth) and one hole is punched through all layers. When unfolded, the number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded three times into an eighth with one hole punched.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='30' y='25' width='30' height='60' fill='rgba(244,63,94,0.10)' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='45' cy='55' r='6' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Three folds create $2^3 = 8$ layers.

**Working.** One punch through $8$ layers gives $8$ holes.

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The net shown is folded into a cube. Which face is opposite face 2?

figure.caption: A cube net: a horizontal row of faces 1, 2, 3, 4 with face 5 above face 2 and face 6 below face 2.
