figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)'><rect x='20' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='140' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='30' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='110' width='40' height='40'/></g><g font-size='16' fill='#334155'><text x='36' y='95'>1</text><text x='76' y='95'>2</text><text x='116' y='95'>3</text><text x='156' y='95'>4</text><text x='76' y='55'>5</text><text x='76' y='135'>6</text></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a straight row of four faces, faces two apart are opposite.

**Working.** With 2 as the front, 1 is left, 3 is right and 4 is back; front (2) is opposite back (4).

**Answer.** 4.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On a standard die, opposite faces sum to $7$. If the top face shows $2$, the bottom face shows

figure.caption: A die with two pips showing on its top face.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='25' y='25' width='70' height='70' rx='8' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='45' cy='45' r='6' fill='#334155'/><circle cx='75' cy='75' r='6' fill='#334155'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite faces of a standard die add to $7$.

**Working.** $7-2=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On a standard die (opposite faces sum to $7$), the top shows $1$ and the front shows $2$. The number on the bottom face is _____ .

figure.caption: A die showing one pip on top and two pips on the front face.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='30,40 80,40 100,25 50,25' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.12)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='30' y='40' width='50' height='55' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='55' cy='32' r='4' fill='#334155'/><circle cx='45' cy='58' r='4' fill='#334155'/><circle cx='65' cy='78' r='4' fill='#334155'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bottom $=7-$ top.

**Working.** $7-1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The plus-shaped net shown is folded into a cube. Which face is opposite face 1?

figure.caption: A plus-shaped cube net: face 1 on top, faces 2, 3, 4 in the middle row, face 5 below 3, and face 6 below 5.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)'><rect x='70' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='30' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='110' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='100' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='140' width='40' height='40'/></g><g font-size='16' fill='#334155'><text x='86' y='45'>1</text><text x='46' y='85'>2</text><text x='86' y='85'>3</text><text x='126' y='85'>4</text><text x='86' y='125'>5</text><text x='86' y='165'>6</text></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fold the vertical strip 1-3-5-6; the faces one step apart along the fold become front/back, top/bottom.

**Working.** Taking 3 as the front, 1 folds to the top and 5 to the bottom, so 1 is opposite 5.

**Answer.** 5.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same plus-shaped net (faces 2, 3, 4 in a row with 1 above 3 and 5, 6 below 3), which face is opposite face 3?

figure.caption: The same plus-shaped cube net with faces 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.08)'><rect x='70' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='30' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='110' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='100' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='140' width='40' height='40'/></g><g font-size='16' fill='#334155'><text x='86' y='45'>1</text><text x='46' y='85'>2</text><text x='86' y='85'>3</text><text x='126' y='85'>4</text><text x='86' y='125'>5</text><text x='86' y='165'>6</text></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Along the vertical strip 1-3-5-6, face 3 has 1 and 5 as neighbours; the remaining strip face 6 ends up opposite 3.

**Working.** With 3 as front, 1 is top, 5 is bottom and 6 wraps to the back, so 3 is opposite 6.

**Answer.** 6.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A cube painted on all faces is cut into $27$ identical small cubes ($3\times3\times3$). The number of small cubes with exactly two painted faces is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube shown with grid lines on its visible faces.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two-face cubes lie along the edges (not corners) of a $3\times3\times3$ cube.

**Working.** A cube has $12$ edges, each contributing one such cube: $12\times1=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same $3\times3\times3$ painted cube cut into $27$ small cubes, the number with exactly one painted face is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube with grid lines on its visible faces.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-face cubes sit at the centre of each face.

**Working.** A cube has $6$ faces, each with one centre cube: $6\times1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same $3\times3\times3$ painted cube cut into $27$ small cubes, the number with exactly three painted faces is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube with its eight corners highlighted.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Three-face cubes are the corner cubes.

**Working.** A cube has $8$ corners, so $8$ small cubes have three painted faces.

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same $3\times3\times3$ painted cube cut into $27$ small cubes, the number with no painted face is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube with the single hidden central cube indicated.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='30' y='50' width='90' height='90' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.06)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='50' x2='60' y2='140' stroke='#334155'/><line x1='90' y1='50' x2='90' y2='140' stroke='#334155'/><line x1='30' y1='80' x2='120' y2='80' stroke='#334155'/><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='120' y2='110' stroke='#334155'/><rect x='60' y='80' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Unpainted cubes are the interior ones, forming a $(3-2)^3$ block.

**Working.** $(3-2)^3 = 1^3 = 1$.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many triangles are there in the square with both diagonals drawn (shown)?

figure.caption: A square with both diagonals drawn, forming four triangular regions.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='15' width='100' height='100' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='15' x2='115' y2='115' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='115' y1='15' x2='15' y2='115' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count the small triangles and the larger ones formed by pairs.

**Working.** There are $4$ small triangles plus $4$ larger triangles (each half of the square), giving $8$.

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many triangles are there in the square with one diagonal drawn (shown)?

figure.caption: A square with a single diagonal, splitting it into two triangles.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='15' width='100' height='100' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='15' x2='115' y2='115' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** One diagonal cuts the square into two equal triangles.

**Working.** Each half is a triangle, so there are $2$.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many squares (of all sizes) are there in the $2\times2$ grid shown?

figure.caption: A 2 by 2 grid of unit squares.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='15' width='100' height='100' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='65' y1='15' x2='65' y2='115' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='65' x2='115' y2='65' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count squares of each size: $1\times1$ and $2\times2$.

**Working.** Four unit squares plus one large square $=4+1=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many squares (of all sizes) are there in the $3\times3$ grid shown?

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 grid of unit squares.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum of squares of sizes $1,2,3$: $3^2+2^2+1^2$.

**Working.** $9+4+1=14$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle has one line drawn from its apex to the midpoint of the base (shown). How many triangles are there in total?

figure.caption: A triangle with a single cevian from the apex to the midpoint of the base.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='80,20 20,110 140,110' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='110' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count the small triangles and the whole.

**Working.** The cevian makes $2$ small triangles, plus the original large triangle $=2+1=3$.

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many rectangles (of all sizes) are there in a strip of three unit cells in a row (shown)?

figure.caption: A horizontal strip divided into three equal unit cells.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='20' width='150' height='40' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='65' y1='20' x2='65' y2='60' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='115' y1='20' x2='115' y2='60' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count rectangles spanning $1$, $2$ or $3$ cells.

**Working.** Single cells $=3$, pairs $=2$, the full strip $=1$; total $=3+2+1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points to the right (east). After rotating it $90^\circ$ clockwise, it points

figure.caption: A horizontal arrow pointing to the right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='120' y2='40' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='120,40 105,32 105,48' fill='#6366f1'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $90^\circ$ clockwise turn sends east to south.

**Working.** Right rotated clockwise becomes downward.

**Answer.** Downward (south).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points upward (north). After rotating it $180^\circ$, it points

figure.caption: A vertical arrow pointing upward.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='40' y2='30' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='40,30 32,45 48,45' fill='#10b981'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $180^\circ$ rotation reverses the direction.

**Working.** Upward becomes downward.

**Answer.** Downward (south).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points to the right (east). After rotating it $90^\circ$ anticlockwise, it points

figure.caption: A horizontal arrow pointing to the right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='120' y2='40' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='120,40 105,32 105,48' fill='#ef4444'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $90^\circ$ anticlockwise turn sends east to north.

**Working.** Right rotated anticlockwise becomes upward.

**Answer.** Upward (north).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The lowercase letter shown, rotated $180^\circ$ in the plane, looks like the letter

figure.caption: The lowercase letter b.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='35' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>b</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $180^\circ$ rotation flips both top-bottom and left-right.

**Working.** 'b' rotated $180^\circ$ becomes 'q'.

**Answer.** q.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The capital letter shown, rotated $180^\circ$ in the plane, looks like

figure.caption: The capital letter N.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='30' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>N</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A letter with $180^\circ$ rotational symmetry is unchanged.

**Working.** 'N' has point symmetry, so rotating $180^\circ$ leaves it looking like 'N'.

**Answer.** N (unchanged).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the dot series shown ($1, 2, 3, \ldots$ dots), the next figure should contain

figure.caption: Three groups of dots containing one, two and three dots respectively.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='95' cy='30' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='95' cy='50' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='165' cy='25' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='165' cy='45' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='165' cy='65' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='210' y='45' font-size='28' fill='#94a3b8'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The count increases by one each step.

**Working.** $1, 2, 3, \ldots$ so the next figure has $4$ dots.

**Answer.** 4 dots.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An arrow rotates $90^\circ$ clockwise each step: up, right, down, ... The next arrow points

figure.caption: Three arrows pointing up, right and down, followed by a question mark.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continue the $90^\circ$ clockwise rotation.

**Working.** After down, a further clockwise quarter-turn points to the left.

**Answer.** Left (west).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square-dot pattern grows as $1, 4, 9, \ldots$ (perfect squares). The number of dots in the next figure is _____ .

figure.caption: Square dot arrangements of 1, 4 and 9 dots.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='25' cy='45' r='4' fill='#6366f1'/><g fill='#6366f1'><circle cx='80' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='95' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='4'/><circle cx='95' cy='50' r='4'/></g><g fill='#6366f1'><circle cx='150' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='55' r='4'/></g><text x='215' y='48' font-size='24' fill='#94a3b8'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The terms are consecutive perfect squares $n^2$.

**Working.** $1=1^2$, $4=2^2$, $9=3^2$, so the next is $4^2=16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a square split into four quadrants, the shaded quadrant moves clockwise each step: top-left, top-right, bottom-right, ... The next shaded quadrant is

figure.caption: Three squares with the shaded quadrant at top-left, top-right and bottom-right respectively.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'><rect x='15' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='95' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='175' y='20' width='40' height='40'/></g><rect x='15' y='20' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='115' y='20' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='195' y='40' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continue the clockwise motion of the shaded quadrant.

**Working.** Top-left, top-right, bottom-right, then bottom-left.

**Answer.** Bottom-left.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Triangle is to $3$ sides as the pentagon shown is to

figure.caption: A regular pentagon.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='60,15 110,52 91,108 29,108 10,52' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The analogy maps a polygon to its number of sides.

**Working.** A pentagon has $5$ sides.

**Answer.** 5 sides.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle is divided into four smaller triangles by joining the midpoints of its sides (shown). The total number of triangles is _____ .

figure.caption: A triangle with its three midpoints joined, forming four small triangles.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='80,15 20,120 140,120' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='67' x2='110' y2='67' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='67' x2='80' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='110' y1='67' x2='80' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count the small triangles and the original.

**Working.** Joining midpoints makes $4$ small triangles; with the original large triangle the total is $4+1=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which two basic shapes make up the house-shaped figure shown?

figure.caption: A house outline: a square with a triangular roof on top.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='35' y='65' width='70' height='60' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='25,65 70,25 115,65' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.12)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Decompose the outline into recognisable shapes.

**Working.** The body is a square and the roof is a triangle.

**Answer.** A square and a triangle.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many straight line segments are needed to form the capital letter shown?

figure.caption: The capital letter E drawn with straight strokes.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='20' x2='30' y2='120' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='30' y1='20' x2='75' y2='20' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='30' y1='70' x2='65' y2='70' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='75' y2='120' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='4'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count each distinct stroke.

**Working.** The letter E has one vertical stroke and three horizontal strokes: $1+3=4$.

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Three quadrants of the square shown are shaded (top-left, top-right, bottom-left). To complete the fully shaded square, the missing piece is

figure.caption: A square with three quadrants shaded and the bottom-right quadrant empty.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='15' width='90' height='90' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='15' y='15' width='45' height='45' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='60' y='15' width='45' height='45' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='15' y='60' width='45' height='45' fill='#6366f1'/><line x1='60' y1='15' x2='60' y2='105' stroke='#334155'/><line x1='15' y1='60' x2='105' y2='60' stroke='#334155'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The completed figure is fully shaded, so the empty quadrant must be filled.

**Working.** The bottom-right quadrant is empty, so shading it completes the square.

**Answer.** Bottom-right shaded.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sector representing $25\%$ of a total is shown shaded on the pie chart. Compute its central angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Pie sector
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 145 80 Z' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='30' y='158' font-size='8'>shaded = 25% of pie</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central angle $=\text{fraction}\times360^\circ$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=0.25\times360=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the values.

**Working.**
$$10+20+30+40=100$$

**Answer.** $100$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The line graph shows a value rising from $20$ to $50$. Compute the percentage increase (%).

figure.caption: Line graph
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><polyline points='40,90 100,75 160,40' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='40' cy='90' r='3' fill='#dc2626'/><circle cx='160' cy='40' r='3' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='45' y='138' font-size='8'>20 -> 50</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\%\text{ increase}=\dfrac{\Delta}{\text{initial}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{50-20}{20}\times100=\frac{30}{20}\times100=150\%$$

**Answer.** $150\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On a pie chart of $800$ students, one sector spans $45^\circ$. Compute the number of students in that sector.

figure.caption: Pie sector of 45 degrees
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 127 38 Z' fill='#16a34a'/><text x='22' y='160' font-size='8'>sector 45 deg, total 800</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count $=\dfrac{\theta}{360}\times\text{total}$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{45}{360}\times800=100$$

**Answer.** $100$ students.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the average of the four bar values shown: $4, 6, 8, 10$.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='75' width='25' height='45'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='45' width='25' height='75'/></g><text x='55' y='138' font-size='8'>4, 6, 8, 10</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average $=\dfrac{\sum x}{n}$.

**Working.**
$$\bar{x}=\frac{4+6+8+10}{4}=\frac{28}{4}=7$$

**Answer.** $7$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the area of the triangle shown with base $6$ and height $4$.

figure.caption: Triangle base and height
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,40' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='120' x2='100' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='90' y='135' font-size='8'>base 6</text><text x='105' y='85' font-size='8'>height 4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\tfrac{1}{2}bh$.

**Working.**
$$A=\tfrac{1}{2}(6)(4)=12$$

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A circle is inscribed in a square of side $10$ (figure). Compute the shaded area outside the circle but inside the square (use $\pi=3.1416$, two decimals).

figure.caption: Inscribed circle in a square
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='25' y='25' width='120' height='120' fill='#2563eb'/><circle cx='85' cy='85' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb'/><text x='30' y='168' font-size='8'>square 10, inscribed circle</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shaded $=$ square area $-$ circle area, with $r=5$.

**Working.**
$$A=10^2-3.1416(5)^2=100-78.54=21.46$$

**Answer.** $21.46$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A square pyramid of unit cubes is built with a $3\times3$ bottom layer, a $2\times2$ middle layer and a single top cube (figure). Compute the total number of cubes.

figure.caption: Three-layer cube stack
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 210 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='40' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='62' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='84' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='51' y='86' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='73' y='86' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='62' y='62' width='22' height='22'/></g><text x='28' y='150' font-size='8'>layers: 3x3, 2x2, 1x1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum the cubes in each layer.

**Working.**
$$N=3^2+2^2+1^2=9+4+1=14$$

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On the clock face shown, the time is $3{:}00$. Compute the smaller angle between the hour and minute hands (degrees).

figure.caption: Clock at 3:00
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='85' y2='28' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='133' y2='80' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='55' y='160' font-size='8'>time = 3:00</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hour mark is $30^\circ$ apart.

**Working.**
$$3\text{ hours}\times30^\circ=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle is divided by a single line from its apex to the midpoint of its base (figure). Count the total number of triangles in the figure.

figure.caption: Triangle with one median
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,30' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='30' x2='100' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><text x='35' y='140' font-size='8'>count the triangles</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count small and combined triangles.

**Working.** Two small right triangles, plus the whole large triangle, give $2+1=3$.

**Answer.** $3$.
